english_query: what makes a good reason for believing something
ugf_response: is true is that the thing fits with what we already know and can be checked by looking at it in different ways. First, we look at how the idea fits with other ideas that have been shown to be true before. If the new idea does not go against any of those old ideas, then it has a strong point. Next, we check whether the person or thing we trust also says the same thing. If they are known by many, or come from someone who knows more than us, that adds power. But we must also think about whether there is any reason why the new idea might be wrong. If there is no such reason, the idea stays good because it follows from other things. So a good reason is one that fits well with other true ideas and helps us see the truth clearly. So my answer is: a good reason to believe something is true comes from things we already know work well together and do not break the way the world works.
